causalAutopsy.notes: registerMachine has always rebuilt the registry entry as { ...(existing ?? {}), status: 'active', ... }, unconditionally forcing status to 'active'. Latent because it only matters when an EXISTING entry is revoked AND something calls registerMachine again for it. It surfaced 2026-06-07 when the stale Mac Mini, revoked earlier, re-registered itself after an update and was flipped back to active, resuming mesh/live-tail chatter. The merge path was made revocation-sticky in an earlier change (mergeRegistry.mergeEntry) but the direct re-register path was never guarded — this closes that symmetric gap. No prior PR regressed it; deepest 'a re-register is always a fresh active machine' assumption surfacing under the revoke+update sequence.
